channel.title: IBD LitMonitor
channel.description: Daily IBD literature monitoring - organized by clinical subfield

item.title: Precision nutrition for the prevention and management of inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Nature reviews. Gastroenterology & hepatology - Review - The rising disease burden of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) parallels the changing dietary landscape accompanying industrialization, underscoring the growing relevance of nutritional science in d...

item.title: Plasma metabolomic and lipidomic profiling reveals distinct molecular signatures and time-dependent remodeling in ulcerative colitis.
item.description: BMC gastroenterology - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease marked by relapsing mucosal inflammation. Recent data indicate that subclinical metabolic disturbances persist even in patients who h...

item.title: Histone H3K27 demethylation drives Crohn's disease inflammation: GSK-J4 as a potential epigenetic therapy.
item.description: Clinical epigenetics - Crohn's disease (CD) is characterised by recurrent gastrointestinal inflammation. Epigenetic modifications, particularly the methylation of histone H3 at lysine 27 trimethylation (H3K27me3), are cr...

item.title: Association between iron status and inflammation markers with GDF15 and BMP6 in inflammatory bowel disease: a single center cross-sectional study.
item.description: BMC gastroenterology - Inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD), including Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC), are chronic disorders with relapsing and remitting disease courses. Several clinical indicators and bi...

item.title: Use Patterns of Novel Janus Kinase Inhibitors After the First Safety Alerts: A Nationwide Register-Based Time Series Analysis.
item.description: Pharmacoepidemiology and drug safety - Janus kinase inhibitors (JAKi) are medicines used to treat inflammatory rheumatic musculoskeletal diseases (IRMDs) and inflammatory bowel diseases (IBDs). In Europe, JAKis received marketing author...

item.title: Clinical Efficacy and Safety Profile of Bimekizumab in Psoriasis: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials.
item.description: Endocrine, metabolic & immune disorders drug targets - Meta-analysis - Bimekizumab, a monoclonal antibody that simultaneously neutralizes interleukin (IL)-17A and IL-17F, may offer therapeutic advantages over other biologics for the treatment of psoriasis. With the av...

item.title: Small Molecules as Advanced Therapy for the Treatment of Inflammatory Bowel Disease, Particularly Regarding Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: Internal medicine (Tokyo, Japan) - Small molecules used to treat inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) are orally administered therapies that offer a rapid onset without immunogenicity. Currently, three Janus kinase (JAK) inhibitors (tof...

item.title: Association between mucus phenotypes, histopathological features, and ultra-high magnification endoscopic findings in ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Scientific reports - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease affecting the colonic mucosa. Changes in mucin phenotype may be associated with mucosal inflammation in UC. However, the relationship...

item.title: Inflammatory Bowel Disease and Complications After Pelvic Organ Prolapse Repair.
item.description: Urogynecology (Philadelphia, Pa.) - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) affects 1.5% of women and results in relapsing inflammation affecting the gastrointestinal (GI) tract, which can lead to intra-abdominal adhesive disease, malnutrit...

item.title: The Longer-Term Impact of Biosimilar Switching Policies in Patients With Inflammatory Bowel Disease in British Columbia, Canada: A Retrospective, Population-Based Study.
item.description: Pharmacoepidemiology and drug safety - In 2019, British Columbia (BC) became the first jurisdiction in North America to introduce a mandatory non-medical switching (NMS) policy requiring patients with Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative...

item.title: Computer-Assisted and Unassisted Optical Diagnosis of Colorectal Polyps: Results from a Prospective Pragmatic Implementation Study.
item.description: Endoscopy - Background and Aims Computer-aided diagnosis (CADx) may support optical diagnosis of diminutive (≤5 mm) colorectal polyps, replacing histopathology. We aimed to describe CADx usage and diagnostic p...

item.title: Primary Sclerosing Cholangitis Recurrence After Liver Transplantation: A Systematic Review and Updated Meta-Analysis.
item.description: Liver international : official journal of the International Association for the Study of the Liver - Meta-analysis - Primary sclerosing cholangitis (PSC) is an immune-mediated cholestatic liver disease. Liver transplantation (LT) remains the only definitive treatment for patients with advanced disease. However, r...

item.title: Case Study of Fulminant Pyoderma Gangrenosum: Conservative Therapy-Cornerstone of Management.
item.description: International medical case reports journal - Case report - Pyoderma gangrenosum (PG) is a rare, immune-mediated neutrophilic dermatosis characterized by painful, rapidly progressive ulceration and a propensity for pathergy. Diagnosis is clinical and often ...

item.title: Lonely minds, inflamed guts: metabolic and circulating protein pathways linking social isolation and loneliness to inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Translational psychiatry - Mounting evidence suggests that psychosocial stressors, such as social isolation and loneliness, contribute to gastrointestinal disorders by disrupting gut-brain interactions. We aim to investigate...

item.title: The cAMP response element modulator (CREM) in Ulcerative colitis: Molecular mechanisms and therapeutic potential.
item.description: Biochemical pharmacology - Review - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is one of the most important chronic inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD), and the aetiology of UC involves the interplay of genetic susceptibility, immune dysregulation, gut m...

item.title: Protein Homeostasis Screen Identifies CK2 Inhibition as a Driver of CARD9 Depletion.
item.description: ACS chemical biology - CARD9 is a genetically validated target for autoimmune disease, with human expression quantitative trait loci (eQTLs) linking elevated CARD9 expression to increased disease risk and lower CARD9 exp...

item.title: [Expert consensus on laboratory diagnosis of inflammatory bowel disease (2026)].
item.description: Zhonghua yu fang yi xue za zhi [Chinese journal of preventive medicine] - In recent years, the incidence of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) in China has shown a significant upward trend. The invasive nature of colonoscopy limits its widespread application in population ...

item.title: Lactate and Akkermansia muciniphila: Emerging Roles in Inflammatory Bowel Disease and Colorectal Cancer.
item.description: Pathobiology : journal of immunopathology, molecular and cellular biology - Review - Lactate levels are significantly elevated in the tissues of patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and colorectal cancer (CRC). In IBD, lactate promotes anti-inflammatory repair by inducing...
